remedies.description: Partial abatement of Minimum Rent: Tenant pays 50% of Minimum Rent beginning 30 days after Landlord's receipt of Tenant's notice of a Protected Use violation, continuing while the violation continues, not to exceed 180 days.
If the violation continues for more than 180 days, Tenant may elect to terminate the Lease on 60 days' prior written notice, or waive further rights and resume paying Minimum Rent.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: This is an exclusive-use (Protected Use) remedy structured like a co-tenancy clause, triggered by another tenant (under a lease entered into after this Lease) operating primarily for the Protected Use. Per Article 20.1(b), Tenant has no right to abatement if the violating tenant is breaching its own lease/license and Landlord pursues Judicial Relief per required cure procedures (i)-(iii).

termination_rights.details: If a Protected Use violation continues for more than 180 days (after the 50% rent abatement period), Tenant may terminate the Lease by giving Landlord 60 days' prior written notice.
Tenant may terminate the Lease if Landlord fails to deliver the Premises on or before December 31, 2021 (Â§3.1).

extraction.verbatim_language: Article 20.1(a): In the event the tenant of any premises in the Shopping Center other than the Premises whose lease is hereafter entered into by Landlord, operates its premises primarily for the Protected Use (a 'violation'), Tenant's sole remedy shall be a partial abatement of Minimum Rent payable hereunder from the thirtieth (30th) day after Landlord receipt of Tenant's notice of such violation and continuing for so long as such violation continues, but not to exceed one hundred eighty (180) days, during which period Tenant shall pay Rent in the amount of fifty percent (50%) of Minimum Rent...If such violation continues for more than one hundred eighty (180) days...Tenant shall either (i) elect to terminate this Lease by giving Landlord sixty (60) days' prior written notice...or (ii) waive any further rights under this stipulation and resume payment of Minimum Rent.

termination_rights.details: One-time tenant right to terminate the lease on 60 days' prior written notice if Landlord's Exclusive Use violation continues past the earlier of 1 year of paying Substitute Rent or expiration of the then-current term; Landlord must reimburse unamortized leasehold improvement costs (less TIA) and reasonable moving expenses.
termination_rights.termination_fee: Landlord reimburses Tenant for unamortized leasehold improvement costs (less TIA) and reasonable moving expenses

extraction.verbatim_language: One-time right for Tenant to terminate Lease (on 60 days' prior written notice) if Landlord's Exclusive Use violation continues past the earlier of 1 year of paying Substitute Rent or expiration of then-current term; Landlord must reimburse unamortized leasehold improvement costs (less TIA) and reasonable moving expenses upon such termination. (Â§1(L-1)(C))

termination_rights.details: Historical/expired one-time right under Original 1987 Lease Section 6 allowing Tenant to terminate if banking office approval from the U.S. Controller of Currency was not obtained by November 10, 1987, with notice due by November 25, 1987; no longer part of the current controlling term.

extraction.verbatim_language: Original 1987 Lease Section 6 gave Tenant a one-time right to terminate if banking office approval from the U.S. Controller of Currency was not obtained by November 10, 1987 (notice by November 25, 1987). This right is historical/expired and not part of the current controlling term.

termination_rights.details: If Required Permits and Approvals for the Expansion are not obtained within 12 months of Tenant's application submission, either Landlord or Tenant may terminate the Third Amendment only (not the Lease) upon written notice.

extraction.verbatim_language: Third Amendment Â§3.B: If Required Permits and Approvals for the Expansion are not obtained within 12 months of Tenant's application submission, either Landlord or Tenant may terminate the Third Amendment (not the Lease) upon written notice.

Go Dark/Recapture: Tenant may not open within 180 days of Actual TI Date or must operate continuously; Landlord has one-time right to recapture and terminate within 60 days if closure exceeds 180 consecutive days, with payment of unamortized Tenant improvements. Also, original construction delay termination right (100+ days past Scheduled TI Date) - rendered inapplicable to Fourth Amendment Extended Term per Fourth Amendment Â§2(f).

remedies.description: Estoppel references tenant right to cease operations ('go dark') upon co-tenancy failure; underlying clause language not found in reviewed excerpts.
Estoppel references tenant right to pay reduced or alternative rent upon co-tenancy failure; underlying clause language not found in reviewed excerpts.
Estoppel references tenant right to terminate the Lease upon co-tenancy failure; underlying clause language not found in reviewed excerpts.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Co-tenancy provision confirmed to exist via Tenant Estoppel (2014-02-26), which certifies no triggering event has occurred; MRI lease profile 'COTENCY' note (11/20/2014) shows 'N/A'. Exact clause terms (named anchors, thresholds, cure periods, remedy order) not found in reviewed excerpts.

termination_rights.details: Tenant Estoppel indicates the Lease contains a co-tenancy provision that could give Tenant the right to terminate the Lease, but the underlying clause terms were not located in reviewed excerpts and no triggering event has occurred as of the estoppel date.

extraction.verbatim_language: MRI lease profile references 'COTENCY' note dated 11/20/2014 with 'N/A' detail; Tenant Estoppel (2014-02-26) states 'no event has occurred giving Tenant the right to cease operations at the Property (i.e. "go dark"), terminate the Lease or pay reduced or alternative rent to Landlord under any of the terms of the Lease, such as a co-tenancy provision.' Exact co-tenancy clause language not found in reviewed excerpts.

remedies.description: Alternate Rent = 2% of Gross Sales (capped at Minimum Rent) plus Additional Rent until the co-tenancy event is cured (applies to both Opening and Ongoing Co-Tenancy Events; Ongoing event must persist 180 days before Alternate Rent applies).
Opening Co-Tenancy: if event continues to first anniversary of would-be Rent Commencement Date (Outside Opening Co-Tenancy Date), Tenant may terminate within 60 days of that date.
Ongoing Co-Tenancy: if Tenant pays Alternate Rent for 12 consecutive full months, Tenant may terminate within 45 days after expiration of the 12th month.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Opening Co-Tenancy (Art. 36.A): TJ Maxx open in Shopping Center, Kohl's open in Project, Target open in Knightdale Marketplace West, and executed Academy Sports lease required at Rent Commencement Date; Tenant may delay opening/RCD if unmet. Ongoing Co-Tenancy (Art. 36.B): TJ Maxx open AND at least one of Best Buy or Academy Sports open. Landlord may cure with a 'Suitable Replacement' permanent national/regional credit tenant (grocery store, or occupying at least 65% of Academy Sports space, 70% of Best Buy space, or 75% of TJ Maxx space, operating retail).

termination_rights.details: If the Opening Co-Tenancy Event remains uncured on the first anniversary of the would-be Rent Commencement Date (Outside Opening Co-Tenancy Date), Tenant may terminate within 60 days of that date (Art. 36.A).
termination_rights.measure_period: first anniversary of would-be Rent Commencement Date; 60-day exercise window

termination_rights.details: If Tenant pays Alternate Rent for 12 consecutive full months due to an Ongoing Co-Tenancy Event, Tenant may terminate within 45 days after expiration of the 12th month (Art. 36.C).
termination_rights.measure_period: 12 consecutive full months of Alternate Rent; 45-day exercise window

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate if a zoning restriction prevents the sale of Protected Merchandise and is not cured within the Zoning Cure Period (Art. 5.A).
Casualty-related termination rights allow Tenant to terminate following damage or destruction of the Premises under Art. 14.
Tenant may terminate if Landlord fails to deliver possession by the Termination Date of September 5, 2024, subject to Tenant/Vendor Delay (Art. 21.F).
Termination rights arise if Approvals are denied or Landlord's Approvals are not obtained within the Approval Period (Art. 21.C).
Tenant may terminate if Landlord fails to deliver the required Non-Disturbance Agreement within 60 days of Lease execution (Art. 23.B).
Tenant may terminate upon eminent domain/taking of the Premises or material portions of the Shopping Center (Art. 16).

extraction.verbatim_language: OPENING CO-TENANCY (Art. 36.A): Tenant requires that on Rent Commencement Date TJ Maxx is open in Shopping Center, Kohl's open in Project, Target open in Knightdale Marketplace West, and Landlord/Academy Sports executed lease for Academy Sports to open. If not met ("Opening Co-Tenancy Event"), Tenant not required to open and Rent Commencement Date delayed to earlier of Tenant's opening date or next April 1/October 1 at least 60 days after event cured. If Tenant elects to open despite the event, Tenant pays Alternate Rent = 2% of Gross Sales (capped at Minimum Rent) plus Additional Rent until event ends. If Opening Co-Tenancy Event continues to first anniversary of what would have been Rent Commencement Date ("Outside Opening Co-Tenancy Date"), Tenant may terminate within 60 days of that date. ONGOING CO-TENANCY (Art. 36.B): Tenant requires throughout Term (after Opening Co-Tenancy satisfied/waived) that (i) TJ Maxx open for business, and (ii) at least one of Best Buy or Academy Sports open for business. If not met for 180 days ("Ongoing Co-Tenancy Event"), Tenant pays Alternate Rent (2% Gross Sales, capped at Minimum Rent) plus Additional Rent until cured. Landlord may cure with a "Suitable Replacement" permanent national/regional tenant (grocery store, or occupying stated % of vacated space: 65% Academy Sports space, 70% Best Buy space, 75% TJ Maxx space). If Tenant pays Alternate Rent for 12 consecutive full months due to Ongoing Co-Tenancy Event, Tenant may terminate within 45 days after expiration of 12th month.

termination_rights.details: If Tenant has not opened the Premises for business within three years of the Effective Date, either Landlord or Tenant may cancel the Lease on 30 days' written notice with no liability to either party.
termination_rights.measure_period: three (3) years from the Effective Date

extraction.verbatim_language: If Tenant has not opened the Premises for business within three (3) years of the Effective Date, either Landlord or Tenant may cancel the Lease on 30 days' written notice, whereupon the Lease becomes null and void with no liability to either party. (Original Lease Â§1(D))

System-of-record cross-check indicates no co-tenancy clause; however, one lease summary references an 'Operating Requirements' condition tied to 60% GLA occupancy by other tenants (excluding Premises and lower level) â this may function similarly to a co-tenancy provision. Requires confirmation.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease on 60 days' written notice if a Protected Use (exclusive) violation continues more than 365 days after Tenant begins paying reduced Rent (Â§20.1).

extraction.verbatim_language: Section 20.1 exclusive-violation remedy allows Tenant to terminate Lease (60 days' written notice) if a Protected Use violation continues more than 365 days after Tenant begins paying reduced Rent. Separately, Â§1.3 'Rent Commencement Date' definition allows Landlord to terminate Lease on 10 days' notice if neither party secures Tenant's Permit within 365 days of Landlord's plan approval. Â§2.3 allows Landlord to recapture Premises if Tenant fails to open for business within 120 days of Rent Commencement Date or goes dark 180 consecutive days (with exceptions).

remedies.description: If the theatre tenant offering first-run movie showings is continuously closed more than 6 months (excluding renovations, Force Majeure, casualty, eminent domain), tenant may elect to pay Co-Tenancy Violation Rent equal to 5% of monthly Gross Sales.
Tenant has the right to terminate if the co-tenancy failure is not cured within 2 years.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Trigger is closure of a theatre tenant offering first-run movie showings; closures due to renovations, Force Majeure, casualty, or eminent domain are excluded. Section 18.28 co-tenancy provisions were SUSPENDED per the Sixth Amendment (until the later of 6/30/2022 or full repayment of Deferred Rent) and further suspended/extended per the Seventh Amendment (until the later of 6/30/2023 or full repayment of Deferred Rent + Additional Deferred Rent). Replacement tenant rules not addressed in reviewed excerpts.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease if the theatre co-tenancy failure (theatre continuously closed more than 6 months) is not cured within 2 years; provision currently suspended per Sixth and Seventh Amendments until the later of 6/30/2023 or full repayment of Deferred Rent and Additional Deferred Rent.

Under the Fourth Amendment, Tenant could terminate if Landlord failed to provide environmental test results, utilities info, title report, ALTA survey, and deed restrictions within 30 days, or if issues could not be resolved within 60 days of due diligence review for the Updated Premises.
Under the First Amendment, Tenant had the right to terminate via a Feasibility Termination Notice if not satisfied with Feasibility Conditions by 1/15/2013 (superseded by later amendments).

extraction.verbatim_language: Co-tenancy tied to a theatre tenant offering first-run movie showings: "if continuously closed more than 6 months (excluding renovations, Force Majeure, casualty, eminent domain), tenant may elect to pay Co-Tenancy Violation Rent equal to 5% of monthly Gross Sales; tenant has right to terminate if not cured within 2 years." Section 18.28 co-tenancy provisions were SUSPENDED per Sixth Amendment (until later of 6/30/2022 or full repayment of Deferred Rent) and further suspended/extended per Seventh Amendment (until later of 6/30/2023 or full repayment of Deferred Rent + Additional Deferred Rent).

remedies.description: Tenant may pay 50% Alternate Rent upon failure of Opening Co-Tenancy Condition (Toys/Babies R Us open and operating; Cabela's open or under construction with 2014 opening) or Ongoing Co-Tenancy Condition (less than 60% of center GLA open and operating).
One-time termination right after 18 months of continuing Opening Co-Tenancy failure.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Opening condition: Toys/Babies R Us open and operating AND Cabela's open or under construction with 2014 opening; Ongoing condition: 60% of center GLA open and operating. Per 2nd Amendment Â§4 'Co-Tenancy Freeze': no Co-Tenancy Failure permitted from 4/1/2020 until later of 12/31/2022 or full repayment of Deferred Rent; time periods for Co-Tenancy Failure calculation restart after freeze expiration. Replacement tenant rules not specified in reviewed excerpts. Note: MRI cross-check flags has_co_tenancy_clause:false, but documents clearly establish the clause.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate if Gross Sales for the 8th Lease Year (ending 9/30/2022) do not exceed $250.00 PSF and Tenant was not Dark more than one week, effective no less than 180 days after written notice sent by 12/29/2022, paying unamortized leasing commissions and TIA.
termination_rights.termination_fee: Unamortized leasing commissions and TIA (amortized straight-line over 10-year Initial Term) as of termination

termination_rights.details: One-time right to terminate if the Opening Co-Tenancy Condition failure continues for 18 months, following the 50% Alternate Rent period.

extraction.verbatim_language: Opening Co-Tenancy Condition: Toys/Babies R Us open and operating; Cabela's open or under construction with 2014 opening. If not met, Tenant may pay 50% Alternate Rent, with one-time termination right after 18 months. Ongoing Co-Tenancy: if less than 60% of center GLA open and operating, Tenant may pay 50% Alternate Rent. Per 2nd Amendment Â§4: 'Co-Tenancy Freeze' - no Co-Tenancy Failure permitted from Effective Date (4/1/2020) until later of 12/31/2022 or full repayment of Deferred Rent; time periods for Co-Tenancy Failure calculation restart after freeze expiration.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: GLA of ground and second floor, excluding Tenant's Premises and lower level (totaling 334,000 SF)

remedies.description: Alternate Rent Right: pay 5% of Gross Sales in lieu of Minimum Rent
Closure Right: close Premises and pay only Minimum Rent/Other Charges
Termination Right if Operating Requirements Failure continues for 12 consecutive months

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Replacement tenant rules not fully detailed in excerpts reviewed; co-tenancy provisions referenced Article 14 generally.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if the Operating Requirements Failure (less than 65% of measured GLA open) continues for 12 consecutive months.
termination_rights.measure_period: 12 consecutive months of Operating Requirements Failure

termination_rights.details: If Gross Sales in the 5th Lease Year are below $4,000,000, Tenant may terminate the Lease with 30 days' notice, paying the unamortized portion of the Construction/Tenant Allowance (amortized over 15 years).
termination_rights.termination_fee: Unamortized portion of Construction/Tenant Allowance (amortized over 15-year period)

extraction.verbatim_language: Operating Requirements: satisfied when tenants with aggregate 65% or more of GLA (ground and second floor, excluding Tenant's Premises and lower level), totaling 334,000 SF, are open for business. Failure of Operating Requirements grants Tenant: (i) Alternate Rent Right (pay 5% of Gross Sales in lieu of Minimum Rent), (ii) Closure Right (close Premises and pay only Minimum Rent/Other Charges), and (iii) Termination Right if Operating Requirements Failure continues for 12 consecutive months.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate if the Delivery Date does not occur within 90 days after Permit issuance, subject to Force Majeure and Tenant Delays (Lease Â§3.1).

Mutual (Landlord/Tenant) termination rights tied to Permit issuance delays under the Rent Commencement Date definition, involving up to a 120+90 day process with 10-day termination notice rights (Lease Â§1.3).

extraction.verbatim_language: Tenant right to terminate if Delivery Date does not occur within 90 days after Permit issuance (subject to Force Majeure and Tenant Delays) (Lease Â§3.1). Landlord/Tenant mutual termination rights tied to Permit issuance delays (Lease Â§1.3 'Rent Commencement Date' definition â up to 120+90 day process with 10-day termination notice rights). Landlord recapture right if Tenant fails to open within 120 days after Rent Commencement Date (Lease Â§2.3).

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: retail use occupancy of the Shopping Center (Art. 40 also triggered if 30% or more of the Shopping Center becomes vacant)

remedies.description: If Target ceases operations, Tenant may pay substitute rent of 5% of Gross Sales in lieu of Base Rent

Tenant may terminate the Lease upon 30 days' notice if Target ceases operations; Art. 40 provides additional termination rights if Shopping Center occupancy falls below 70% retail use or 30% or more becomes vacant

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Target's continued operation stated as material inducement to Tenant entering the Lease. Replacement-tenant rules not fully detailed in reviewed excerpts. Art. 40 adds occupancy-based termination triggers (below 70% retail use occupancy, or 30%+ vacancy).

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease upon 30 days' notice if Target ceases operations (Art. 38), or if Shopping Center occupancy falls below 70% retail use or 30% or more of the Shopping Center becomes vacant (Art. 40).

Original Lease Art. 46 granted Tenant the right to close and terminate upon 60 days' notice after the first lease year, but Fifth Amendment Â§2 supersedes this right â Tenant has no right to terminate prior to the Revised Expiration Date of 9/30/2030.

extraction.verbatim_language: Article 38 of the Original Lease provides co-tenancy rights tied to Target anchor tenant's continued operation as a material inducement to Tenant entering the Lease; if Target ceases operations, Tenant may pay substitute rent (5% of Gross Sales) in lieu of Base Rent, or terminate the Lease upon 30 days' notice. Article 40 provides additional termination rights if occupancy in the Shopping Center falls below 70% retail use or 30% or more of the Shopping Center becomes vacant.

remedies.description: Tenant may elect to delay rent commencement or pay 50% of rent while the Co-Tenancy Condition is not met.
Tenant may terminate if the Co-Tenancy Condition remains unmet after 18 months.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Anchor list substituted/updated by First Amendment (originally 3 of 5: Costco, Regal Cinemas, Rooms to Go, Toys/Babies R Us and Cabela's; now 3 of 6). Landlord has demonstrated flexibility to substitute anchors via amendment. Tenant may alternatively elect to delay rent commencement instead of paying reduced rent.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease if the Co-Tenancy Condition (3 of 6 named anchors open, as amended) remains unmet for 18 months; right stems from original lease and was not restated/confirmed in the First Amendment.

extraction.verbatim_language: Co-Tenancy Condition (as amended by First Amendment): requires three of the following six anchor tenants open: Costco, Cabela's, Regal Cinemas, Urban Air, Rooms to Go, and Dave & Buster's (originally: Costco, Regal Cinemas, Rooms to Go, Toys/Babies R Us and Cabela's - three of five). Per original lease, Tenant may elect to delay rent commencement or pay 50% rent if condition not met, with termination right after 18 months if co-tenancy remains unmet.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: 250,000 SF aggregate retail floor area; alternatively at least three 20,000+ SF retail stores open

remedies.description: Tenant may pay Alternate Rent equal to the lesser of minimum rent or 2% of Gross Sales until the Inducement Event is cured
If the Inducement Event continues 395+ days, Tenant may terminate the Lease

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Opening co-tenancy: Demised Premises could not open until Kohl's (Inducement Tenant, 120,327 SF) opened for business under a lease with 10+ years remaining, non-cancelable except for casualty/condemnation/certain events. Ongoing Inducement Event failure triggered if aggregate retail floor area drops below 250,000 SF or fewer than three 20,000+ SF retail stores are open for 30+ days. Replacement tenant rules not specified in reviewed excerpts.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if the Inducement Event (co-tenancy failure) continues for 395 or more days.
Tenant may terminate in the event of casualty pursuant to Article X.
Tenant may terminate in the event of eminent domain/condemnation pursuant to Article XI.
Tenant may terminate if Landlord fails to meet the Deadline Date for completion of Landlord's construction.
Tenant may terminate as an exclusive remedy if a Competing Use violation continues uncured for 270 or more days.

extraction.verbatim_language: Inducement Store requirement: Demised Premises could not open until Kohl's (Inducement Tenant, 120,327 SF) opened for business under a lease with 10+ years remaining, non-cancelable except for casualty/condemnation/certain events. If Inducement Event fails (aggregate retail floor area drops below 250,000 SF or fewer than three 20,000+ SF retail stores open for 30+ days), Tenant may pay Alternate Rent (lesser of minimum rent or 2% of Gross Sales) until cured; if the Inducement Event continues 395+ days, Tenant may terminate the Lease.

remedies.description: If Opening Inducement Condition (Target plus at least 3 of Ross, Kohl's, PetSmart, or Michaels open and operating) is not satisfied, Tenant may pay Alternate Rent of 2% of Gross Sales in lieu of Minimum Rent (Lease Â§4.3(B), Â§4.7).

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Two-tier structure: Opening Inducement Condition requires Target AND at least 3 of [Ross, Kohl's, PetSmart, Michaels] open and operating; Ongoing Inducement Condition requires Target AND at least 2 of [PetSmart, Ross, Michaels, Academy Sports] operating. Replacement tenant rules not specified in reviewed excerpts. HomeGoods is itself a Required Co-Tenant under Ross's separate lease (Sections 1.7.1/6.1.3).

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease on 30 days' notice if a violation of Tenant's Exclusive Use or Prohibited Use continues more than 180 consecutive days after Tenant first commenced paying Alternate Rent, subject to a stay if Landlord is diligently enforcing against the Rogue Tenant (MOL Â¶4(C)).
termination_rights.measure_period: 180 consecutive days of continuing exclusive/prohibited use violation after Tenant commenced paying Alternate Rent

extraction.verbatim_language: Opening Inducement Condition requires Target and at least three of: Ross, Kohl's, PetSmart, or Michaels open and operating; Ongoing Inducement Condition requires Target and at least two of: PetSmart, Ross, Michaels, or Academy Sports operating. If Opening Inducement Condition not satisfied, Tenant may pay Alternate Rent (2% of Gross Sales) in lieu of Minimum Rent (Lease Â§4.3(B), Â§4.7).

Landlord had the right to terminate the Extension Period (11/1/2026-10/31/2028) by giving Tenant 180 days written notice prior to commencement of the Extension Period (i.e., prior to 11/1/2026). This right applied to the Third Amendment's Extension Period; no similar termination right is stated for the current Additional Extension Period (2028-2033) in the Fourth Amendment.

remedies.description: Tenant may pay reduced rent equal to 5% of Gross Sales or the lesser of base/percentage/adjustment rent
Tenant may go dark paying only base rent
Tenant may terminate the Lease after 180 consecutive days of continuing co-tenancy violation

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Replacement tenant rules not fully reviewed in abstract; triggered when less than 75% of Shopping Center Rentable Area is open OR when Named Tenants (Whole Foods and Target) are not open.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if a co-tenancy violation (less than 75% of Shopping Center Rentable Area open or Whole Foods and Target not open) continues for 180 consecutive days.
termination_rights.measure_period: 180 consecutive days of continuing co-tenancy violation

extraction.verbatim_language: Co-tenancy provision (Article Seven) triggered when less than 75% of Shopping Center Rentable Area is open or when Whole Foods and Target (Named Tenants) are not open; allows tenant to pay reduced rent (5% of Gross Sales or lesser of base/percentage/adjustment rent) or go dark paying only base rent; termination right after 180 consecutive days of continuing violation.

named_tenants: Toby Keith's I Love This Bar & Grill

remedies.description: If Pre-Construction Co-Tenancy Condition (executed leases with at least 5 of the specified co-tenants or suitable replacements) not satisfied by June 30, 2013, Tenant may terminate the Lease or waive the requirement.

If Opening Co-Tenancy Requirements (access roads including roundabout completed; Building D shell completed; Montgomery Ward building demolished; two additional co-tenants from specified list or suitable replacements) not met by Rent Commencement Date, Tenant may delay opening.
Alternatively, if Opening Co-Tenancy Requirements not met by Rent Commencement Date, Tenant may open and pay 50% Alternative Rent.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Two-stage clause: (1) Pre-Construction condition requiring executed leases with at least 5 of 12 named co-tenants or suitable replacements acceptable to Tenant by June 30, 2013; (2) Opening requirements including physical milestones (access roads/roundabout, Building D shell, Montgomery Ward demolition) plus two additional co-tenants from the list or suitable replacements. Replacement tenants permitted for both stages if acceptable to Tenant. No ongoing/operating co-tenancy identified.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if the Pre-Construction Co-Tenancy Condition (executed leases with at least 5 of the specified co-tenants or suitable replacements) is not satisfied by June 30, 2013.

extraction.verbatim_language: Pre-Construction Co-Tenancy Condition: Tenant not required to accept possession unless Landlord has executed leases with not less than five (5) specified co-tenants (Firebirds Wood Fired Grill, Yard House, Dave & Buster's, Parmida Home, Toby Keith's I Love This Bar & Grill, Blu Sushi, Bonefish Grill, Chuy's, Cabela's, Tucanos, Brio Tuscan Grill, Bravo! Cucina Italiana) or suitable replacements acceptable to Tenant. If Co-Tenancy Condition not satisfied by June 30, 2013, Tenant may terminate or waive requirement. Opening Co-Tenancy Requirements: access roads including roundabout completed; Building D shell completed; Montgomery Ward building demolished; two additional co-tenants from specified list or suitable replacements obtained. If Opening Co-Tenancy Requirements not met by Rent Commencement Date, Tenant may delay opening or pay 50% Alternative Rent.
No co-tenancy conditions specified per file summary of base Lease. No termination/kick-out right identified in reviewed documents.

remedies.description: Tenant may pay 50% Base Rent ("Substitute Rent") for up to 12 months if three (3) of four Key Tenants are simultaneously closed
Tenant may terminate with 30 days notice if three (3) of four Key Tenants are simultaneously closed

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Trigger is 3 of 4 named Key Tenants simultaneously closed, excluding closures due to casualty, condemnation, or force majeure. Substitute Rent capped at 12 months. Based on secondary, not-fully-reviewed summary; full operative article text not included in reviewed excerpts.

termination_rights.details: If three (3) of four Key Tenants (Target, Ross, Bed Bath & Beyond, Michaels) are simultaneously closed (excluding casualty, condemnation, force majeure), Tenant may terminate the lease with 30 days notice.
Tenant may terminate under Section 42 of the Lease (referenced in the COVID First Amendment Â§1(c)(i)); full text of Article 42 was not included in the reviewed excerpts so terms are unknown.

extraction.verbatim_language: Per secondary (not-fully-reviewed) summary of current lease: Co-tenancy threshold - if three (3) of four Key Tenants (Target, Ross, Bed Bath & Beyond, Michaels) are simultaneously closed (excluding casualty, condemnation, force majeure), Tenant may pay 50% Base Rent ("Substitute Rent") for up to 12 months or terminate with 30 days notice. Full operative article text not included in reviewed excerpts.

remedies.description: Prior to Delivery Date: Tenant may elect not to accept delivery, or accept delivery and not be obligated to open
Rent abatement (Alternate Rent) during Co-Tenancy Failure
Termination right (Blackout Period Election / Election B) if key tenants are absent or occupancy falls below 60%, after 3-month cure period from Election Notice

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Applies if, prior to the Delivery Date, any Key Co-Tenant is not open and operating or Occupancy is less than 60%. Replacement-tenant rules not specified in reviewed excerpts (NOT FULLY REVIEWED). Cure period is 3 months from Election Notice.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate if Gross Sales do not exceed $1,400,000 during any 12-month period prior to the 60th month of the Term; MRI options table shows TRM option effective 4/30/2022 with notice by 6/30/2022.
termination_rights.measure_period: any 12-month period prior to the 60th month of the Term

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate (Blackout Period Election / Election B) if Key Co-Tenants are absent or Center occupancy falls below 60% and the failure continues beyond the 3-month cure period from Election Notice.

Side letter references additional termination rights for failure to deliver premises, co-tenancy failures, and failure to obtain construction permits (side letter to Core Properties - not fully reviewed).

extraction.verbatim_language: Sec 8; If, prior to the Delivery Date, (a) any of the Key Co-Tenants are not open and operating in the Center or (b) Occupancy is less than 60%, Tenant may elect to not accept delivery or accept delivery and not be obligated to open. Key Co-Tenants referenced include Regal Cinemas, Cabela's, Nordstrom Rack, Pier One. Co-Tenancy Failure provisions allow rent abatement (Alternate Rent) or termination rights (Blackout Period Election / Election B) if key tenants are absent or occupancy falls below 60%. Co-Tenancy Cure Period: 3 months from Election Notice.

termination_rights.details: Tenant (and Landlord) may terminate in the event of a material casualty (more than 1/3 of replacement value of the leased premises or Building materially adversely impacting Tenant's ability to operate) occurring during the last two years of the then-current term, exercisable within 90 days of receipt of casualty notice.
If Landlord fails to meet casualty-restoration deadlines (subject to force majeure), Tenant may terminate on written notice.

extraction.verbatim_language: 2020 Letter Agreement references co-tenancy in context of a release: Tenant 'hereby forever waives and releases Landlord from any claims arising under or in connection with the Lease which accrued prior to the date hereof which relate to co-tenancy under the Lease' (with respect to the Deferment Period only). Casualty: Landlord and Tenant each have right to terminate in event of material loss (more than 1/3 of replacement value of leased premises or Building materially adversely impacting Tenant's ability to operate) during last two years of then-current term; must be exercised within 90 days of receipt of casualty notice. Also, if Landlord fails to meet casualty-restoration deadlines (subject to force majeure), Tenant may terminate on written notice.

Failure of Tenant to open the Premises for business fully fixtured, stocked and staffed and otherwise in accordance with the Lease terms by April 30, 2024 (for reasons other than Force Majeure) shall constitute an 'Event of Default' under the Lease.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease if the drive-through lane is not constructed within 90 days, per the Original Lease summary.

extraction.verbatim_language: Tenant right to terminate if drive-through lane not constructed within 90 days (per Original Lease summary).

termination_rights.details: Historical right to terminate if Tenant failed to obtain Franchisor approval within 30 days of Lease execution (Rider B Art. 42); tied to the Original Term and nullified for the Extended Term by First Amendment Â§8.

Historical right to terminate if Tenant failed to obtain necessary permits within 90 days of Lease execution (extendable by 15 days) (Rider B Art. 43); tied to the Original Term and nullified for the Extended Term by First Amendment Â§8.

extraction.verbatim_language: Original Lease provided Tenant termination rights for (1) failure to obtain Franchisor approval within 30 days of Lease execution (Art. 42) and (2) failure to obtain necessary permits within 90 days of execution, extendable by 15 days (Art. 43). These were tied to the Original Term and are historical/likely expired; First Amendment Â§8 nullifies any renewal, termination, or expansion options/rights under the Existing Lease going forward into the Extended Term.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: 90% of Target space plus two additional anchor tenants open and operating

remedies.description: If On-Going Co-Tenancy unsatisfied for 12 months, Tenant pays Alternative Rent of 2% of Gross Sales, capped at Minimum Rent
If On-Going Co-Tenancy unsatisfied for 24 months, or Initial Co-Tenancy Requirement not met within 6 months, Tenant may terminate with 60 days' notice

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Distinguishes Initial Co-Tenancy Requirement (opening test: Target 120,000 SF discount dept store; Bed Bath & Beyond/Linens 'N Things/HomeGoods 20,000 SF; TJ Maxx/Marshalls/Ross 20,000 SF; OfficeMax/Staples/Home Depot 20,000 SF; plus one additional tenant from specified list at 20,000 SF) from On-Going Co-Tenancy (90% of Target space plus two additional anchor tenants open and operating). Replacement tenants permitted per named alternative anchors in each category. NOT FULLY REVIEWED for post-2005 amendment status.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate with 60 days' notice if On-Going Co-Tenancy is unsatisfied for 24 months, or if the Initial Co-Tenancy Requirement is not met within 6 months.
termination_rights.measure_period: 24 months of continuing On-Going Co-Tenancy failure, or 6 months for Initial Co-Tenancy Requirement

termination_rights.details: Original lease gave Tenant termination rights if Landlord failed to meet construction deadlines or materially deviated from approved plans; largely historical/superseded by completion.

extraction.verbatim_language: Initial Co-Tenancy Requirement: Target (120,000 SF discount dept store), Bed Bath & Beyond/Linens 'N Things/HomeGoods (20,000 SF), TJ Maxx/Marshalls/Ross (20,000 SF), OfficeMax/Staples/Home Depot (20,000 SF), and one additional tenant from specified list (20,000 SF). On-Going Co-Tenancy: 90% of Target space plus two additional anchor tenants must be open and operating; if unsatisfied for 12 months, Tenant pays Alternative Rent (2% of Gross Sales, capped at Minimum Rent); if unsatisfied for 24 months or Initial Co-Tenancy not met within 6 months, Tenant may terminate with 60 days' notice.

termination_rights.details: Casualty-based termination: if the Leased Premises structure is substantially damaged (50%+ of replacement cost) by fire/casualty not caused by Tenant, Tenant cannot reasonably operate, and Landlord fails to substantially restore within 1 year of the casualty, Tenant may terminate on 30 days' notice given within 30 days after the 1-year period, subject to Landlord's right to nullify by completing restoration within that 30-day window.

extraction.verbatim_language: Casualty-based termination right only: If structure of Leased Premises is substantially damaged (50%+ of replacement cost) due to fire/casualty not caused by Tenant, and Tenant cannot reasonably operate, and Landlord fails to substantially restore within 1 year of casualty, Tenant may terminate on 30 days' notice given within 30 days after the 1-year period (subject to Landlord's right to complete restoration within that 30-day window to nullify termination). No sales-based or co-tenancy kick-out right identified. (Lease Art. 13(E))

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease upon 30 days' written notice if the Lease Term has not commenced within 24 months of the Lease date, provided the delay is not due to the terminating party's bad faith (Art. 2.2).

extraction.verbatim_language: Tenant right to terminate if Lease Term has not commenced within 24 months of the Lease date (for reasons other than bad faith of terminating party), upon 30 days' written notice (Art. 2.2). Also, Landlord may recapture/terminate if Tenant fails to open for business within 90 days of Rent Commencement Date (Art. 2.3), or if Tenant ceases operating for more than 180 consecutive days (Art. 5.2(b)). No tenant sales-based kickout right identified.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: Shopping Center Floor Area open and operating for retail sales

remedies.description: Tenant may terminate the Lease after twelve (12) months of continuing Failed Cotenancy at no charge and receive reimbursement of Tenant Improvement Costs.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Failed Cotenancy triggered if, for a consecutive 12-month period after the Opening Date, less than 65% of Shopping Center Floor Area is open and operating for retail sales OR fewer than two Anchor Tenants are operating (anchors are generic, not named). Per Second Amendment, cotenancy rights were waived until January 31, 2022 (expiration of Repayment Period), and any Failed Cotenancy or Rent Reduction Period is tolled during deferment and repayment periods. Replacement-tenant cure mechanics not fully reviewed.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease after twelve (12) months of a continuing Failed Cotenancy (less than 65% of Shopping Center Floor Area open or fewer than two Anchor Tenants operating) at no charge, with reimbursement of Tenant Improvement Costs; right waived/tolled until January 31, 2022 per Second Amendment.
termination_rights.measure_period: any consecutive twelve (12) month period after the Opening Date
termination_rights.termination_fee: None; Tenant receives reimbursement of Tenant Improvement Costs

extraction.verbatim_language: FAILED COTENANCY: A 'Failed Cotenancy' occurs if, at any time after the Opening Date, for a consecutive twelve (12) month period, less than 65% of Shopping Center Floor Area is open and operating for retail sales or fewer than two Anchor Tenants are operating; upon Failed Cotenancy, Tenant may terminate after twelve months at no charge and receive reimbursement of Tenant Improvement Costs. Per Second Amendment: 'Cotenancy rights waived until January 31, 2022 (expiration of Repayment Period); any Failed Cotenancy or Rent Reduction Period tolled during deferment and repayment periods.'

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: ground floor and second floor GLA (excluding Premises and lower level)

remedies.description: Tenant may pay Alternate Rent equal to 60% of Minimum Rent if the Operating Requirements fail for 365+ consecutive days
Tenant may terminate the Lease if the Operating Requirements Failure continues for 365+ consecutive days after Tenant has paid Alternate Rent for 365+ consecutive days

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Operating Requirements satisfied when tenants occupying 60%+ of ground floor and second floor GLA (excluding Premises and lower level) are open for business. Replacement-tenant rules not specified in reviewed excerpts. Clause referenced and modified by COVID-19 Rent Amendment Â§2(c).

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if the Operating Requirements Failure continues for 365+ consecutive days after Tenant has already paid Alternate Rent (60% of Minimum Rent) for 365+ consecutive days.
termination_rights.measure_period: 365+ consecutive days of continuing failure after 365+ consecutive days of paying Alternate Rent

extraction.verbatim_language: Operating Requirements: Satisfied when tenants occupying 60% or more of ground floor and second floor GLA (excluding Premises and lower level) are open for business. Tenant can pay Alternate Rent (60% of Minimum Rent) if requirements fail for 365+ consecutive days, and may terminate if failure continues for 365+ days after paying Alternate Rent for 365+ days. Alternate Rent Right under Section 14.4 of the Lease.

remedies.description: If co-tenants cease operations for 120 days (60 days if both close), base rent reduces 50% for up to 12 months.
Tenant may terminate after 1 year of continued co-tenancy violation.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Requires Target or Home Depot AND one national/regional retailer occupying >20,000 sf. Cure period is 60 days if both co-tenants close. Replacement by comparable major retailer (Target, Home Depot, or similar) permitted per Exhibit I. A Rent Commencement Co-Tenancy Requirement in Exhibit I also affects the Commencement Date under Lease Â§2.1(b); opening vs continuing co-tenancy distinction applies.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease after the co-tenancy violation continues for 1 year following the applicable cure period.
Tenant may terminate after an exclusive-use violation continues for 6 or more months, per the Exclusive Use exhibit.

extraction.verbatim_language: Initial Co-Tenants required: Target or Home Depot AND one national/regional retailer occupying >20,000 sf; if co-tenants cease operations for 120 days (60 days if both close), base rent reduces 50% for up to 12 months; tenant may terminate after 1 year of continued violation. Rent Commencement Co-Tenancy Requirement set forth in Exhibit I also affects the Commencement Date under Lease Â§2.1(b).

Landlord may terminate the Lease upon 20 days' advance written notice if Tenant is not required to continuously operate (because 50%+ of Shopping Center GLA excluding Premises has ceased operations for 180+ consecutive days) and the Premises remains closed for business for more than 60 days; Tenant may cancel Landlord's termination by reopening within 10 days of receipt of termination notice and remaining open continuously for 1 year thereafter. Also, Opening Date remedy: if Tenant is not open for business within 45 days after the Opening Date, Landlord may terminate upon written notice.

Section 1.09 (Key Tenants), Section 1.10 (Absence of Key Tenants - cancellation right), and Section 1.11 (Tenant Rent During Key Tenant Absence - rent abatement to 4% of Gross Sales) of the Original Lease are hereby deleted in their entirety and shall be of no further force or effect.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: remaining GLA of Shopping Center (excluding Leased Premises and Key Tenant space)

remedies.description: Tenant may elect to pay Substitute Rent (3% of Gross Sales, no Gross Sales Base) in lieu of Base Rent, Tax Rent, Fixed CAM and Percentage Rent, retroactive to date of failure; right continues until earlier of threshold being met or 24 months after date of failure.

After 6 calendar months of continued failure, Tenant may terminate on 30 days' notice; right continues until earlier of Co-Tenancy Threshold being met or 18 months after expiration of the 6-month period. Also a 30-day termination right within 30 days after expiration of the 24-month Substitute Rent period.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Key Tenant = Target or up to 2 comparable national/regional soft goods retail successors (one of two may be hard goods) occupying not less than 75% of Target's space in aggregate, each under a single trade name. Threshold fails if Key Tenant closed (excluding permitted temp closures up to 60 days for casualty/condemnation/renovation/repairs) OR more than 35% of remaining GLA (excluding Leased Premises and Key Tenant space) not open. Tenant must be open/operating and not in default beyond notice/cure to invoke. Separate Art. 48 Opening Co-Tenancy: if Key Tenant and/or Ross, Michaels, or Rack Room not open by Rent Commencement Date, Tenant need not open; if it opens, pays Substitute Rent (3%); full Rent resumes after 24 months regardless; termination right available months 22-28 if Opening Condition unsatisfied. Landlord disputed Tenant's April 2020 invocation, asserting Sec. 46(B) prerequisites unmet and Force Majeure does not excuse Rent.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate on 10 days' notice if Landlord fails to tender possession in required condition by March 4, 2019; void if Landlord tenders within the 10-day period (Art. 1(D)).

Tenant may terminate on 30 days' notice (within 30 days after the 9-month restoration period) if the structure is substantially damaged (50%+ of replacement cost) by casualty not caused by Tenant, Tenant cannot reasonably operate, and Landlord fails to substantially restore within 9 months (Art. 13(E)).

Art. 46: after 6 calendar months of continued co-tenancy failure, Tenant may terminate on 30 days' notice; right continues until earlier of threshold being met or 18 months after expiration of the 6-month period, plus a 30-day termination right within 30 days after expiration of the 24-month Substitute Rent period.
termination_rights.measure_period: 6 calendar months of continued co-tenancy failure

termination_rights.details: Art. 48 Opening Co-Tenancy: Tenant may terminate during months 22-28 after the original scheduled Rent Commencement Date if the Opening Condition (Key Tenant and/or Ross, Michaels, or Rack Room open) remains unsatisfied.
termination_rights.measure_period: months 22-28 after original scheduled Rent Commencement Date

termination_rights.details: Art. 49: Tenant may terminate if Gross Sales do not exceed $846,450.00 during months 11-22 following the Opening Date, with notice by end of the 28th month following Opening Date, no default, and a $27,588.00 termination payment; effective 60 days after Landlord receives notice.
Art. 25: either party may terminate on 30 days' notice if Landlord is unable to deliver the Leased Premises within 1 year after Landlord's execution of the Lease.
Art. M-1: Tenant termination rights upon violation of Tenant's exclusive use protections (see exclusives).

remedies.description: Rent abatement calculated per Co-Tenancy Rent Reduction formula (Comparison Period revenues Ã· Base Period revenues) after 24-month cure period
Right to go dark and expanded use rights after 24 months without replacement tenants

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Replacement department store per Phase III expansion permitted per Original Lease definitions; First Amendment adds co-tenancy requirement of 3 sit-down restaurants (e.g., Cheesecake Factory, Yardhouse, Bravo!, Tucanos, Blackfinn, Vida, Firebirds)

extraction.verbatim_language: Co-Tenancy Event triggered if two of three anchor tenants (Parisian, Montgomery Wards, J.B. White or replacement department store per Phase III expansion) not open/operating. Tenant receives rent abatement calculated by Co-Tenancy Rent Reduction formula (Comparison Period revenues Ã· Base Period revenues) after 24-month cure period; right to go dark and expanded use rights after 24 months without replacement tenants.

named_tenants: Anchor Tenant in Retail H or Retail I (14,800 sq.ft.)
co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: Landlord's Parcel Leasable Floor Area occupied by operating retailers (excluding Store)

remedies.description: During a Reduced Occupancy Period, Tenant may pay Substitute Rent equal to 2% of Gross Sales in lieu of base rent.
Tenant may terminate the Lease if Co-Tenancy Requirements remain unsatisfied for 12+ consecutive months.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Required Co-Tenants include Target (123,000 sq.ft.) plus four generically identified anchor tenants of specified sizes; occupancy test excludes Tenant's Store. Landlord must deliver co-tenancy certification/occupancy reports within 30 days of Tenant request. Replacement-tenant pre-approval not specified in reviewed excerpts.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if Co-Tenancy Requirements remain unsatisfied for 12 or more consecutive months (Reduced Occupancy Period), in lieu of or in addition to paying Substitute Rent.

extraction.verbatim_language: Required Co-Tenants: Target (123,000 sq.ft.), Anchor Tenant (25,000 sq.ft.), Anchor Tenant (23,000 sq.ft.), Anchor Tenant (18,000 sq.ft.), Anchor Tenant in Retail H or Retail I (14,800 sq.ft.). Sixty percent (60%) of Landlord's Parcel Leasable Floor Area must be occupied by operating retailers (excluding Store). Reduced Occupancy Period triggered if requirements not met; Tenant may pay Substitute Rent (2% of Gross Sales) or terminate after 12 consecutive months of non-compliance. Landlord must deliver co-tenancy certification/occupancy reports to Tenant within 30 days of request.

remedies.description: NOT FULLY REVIEWED - MRI cross-check flags co-tenancy clause exists, but operative language and remedies were not included in reviewed excerpts; ground lease summary references co-tenancy-style restrictions (no competing steakhouse restaurants or off-price apparel stores). Remedy ladder unknown.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Clause flagged as existing (has_co_tenancy_clause=true) but not fully reviewed; possibly within Lease Article 18 or related exhibits. Referenced restrictions appear to be use/exclusivity restrictions (no competing steakhouse restaurants or off-price apparel stores) rather than a traditional anchor co-tenancy test. Named tenants, occupancy tests, cure periods, and remedies are unknown pending full review.

extraction.verbatim_language: NOT FULLY REVIEWED - MRI cross-check flags has_co_tenancy_clause=true; original ground lease summary references 'co-tenancy' restrictions (e.g., no competing steakhouse restaurants or off-price apparel stores) but full operative language/remedies not included in reviewed excerpts.

remedies.description: Alternative Rent equal to 5% of Gross Sales after Continuing Co-Tenancy Failure persists 30 consecutive days
Tenant may terminate if Continuing Co-Tenancy Failure continues beyond 12 months

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Named Co-Tenants may be satisfied by a Comparable Replacement Tenant (specifics not fully reviewed). Clause is a continuing co-tenancy requirement. NOTE: system-of-record cross-check flags has_co_tenancy_clause: false, but reviewed lease summary documents the clause; abstracted per source hierarchy.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease if a Continuing Co-Tenancy Failure (Target and Whole Foods or Comparable Replacement Tenant open; 70% ground floor occupancy) continues beyond 12 months.
Tenant may terminate if repair/restoration following fire or casualty exceeds 12 months.
Tenant may terminate if more than 10% of the Premises is condemned under eminent domain.

extraction.verbatim_language: Continuing Co-Tenancy Requirements: Target and Whole Foods (Named Co-Tenants) or Comparable Replacement Tenant must be open; minimum 70% of ground floor occupancy required. Continuing Co-Tenancy Failure triggers Alternative Rent (5% of Gross Sales) after 30 consecutive days; 12-month Termination Trigger allows Tenant to terminate if failure continues.

Lease may be terminated by Landlord upon 10 days' written notice if neither party has secured the required Permit for Tenant's Initial Work within 210 days following Lease execution (extended by a 90-day Landlord-assist period), per the Rent Commencement Date definition. Also, if the Lease Term has not commenced within 12 months of the Lease date, Landlord may terminate on 30 days' written notice (Â§2.2).

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate if Government Approvals (permits/licenses) are not obtained within 60 days, or if permits are revoked during the term due to Landlord conduct (per earlier draft/ground lease Â§17); confirm carryover into the final executed 8/11/2015 lease.

Tenant is entitled to equitable rent abatement if interference with operations (including hazardous substance interference) exceeds 24 hours, with an option to terminate if the interference continues for 6 or more months (per earlier draft/ground lease Â§18); confirm carryover into the final executed 8/11/2015 lease.
Tenant's right of early termination in specified circumstances tied to delivery delays.

extraction.verbatim_language: Tenant's Right of Early Termination in specified circumstances tied to delivery delays and hazardous substance interference; earlier draft/ground lease terms reference Tenant right to terminate if Government Approvals (permits/licenses) not obtained within 60 days (Â§17), or if permits revoked during term due to Landlord conduct; right to equitable rent abatement if interference with operations exceeds 24 hours, with termination option if interference continues 6+ months (Â§18).

termination_rights.details: Tenant could terminate if Landlord failed to deliver the Leased Premises with Landlord's Work substantially complete by March 15, 2007 (notice required before April 1, 2007); this delivery-related right applied only to original lease commencement and is now expired/moot.

extraction.verbatim_language: Original Lease Article 1(D): Tenant had right to terminate if Landlord did not tender Leased Premises with Landlord's Work substantially complete by March 15, 2007 (notice required before April 1, 2007) - this was a delivery-related termination right applicable to original lease commencement only, now expired/moot. First Amendment Â§10 (Tenant's Right to Go Dark/Landlord's Right of Recapture) allows Tenant to cease business operations without default, and grants Landlord recapture right after 90 days of closure (subject to Tenant's right to vitiate recapture by reopening within 30 days of notice).

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate early if the Delivery Date is not achieved by July 1, 2021 (as extended for Pandemic Delay).
Lease automatically terminates if Delivery is not achieved by January 1, 2022.
Tenant may terminate early if required SNDAs are not provided within 60 days of the Effective Date.
Tenant may terminate early if the Recognition Agreement is not provided within 60 days of the Effective Date.

Go-Dark termination right: Tenant may terminate if it does not open for business as a Target store within 12 months of Rent Commencement or goes dark for 12 consecutive months, subject to liquidated damages of $1,795,620 for failure to open.
termination_rights.termination_fee: $1,795,620 liquidated damages for failure to open

extraction.verbatim_language: Early termination right if Delivery Date not achieved by July 1, 2021 (extended for Pandemic Delay); automatic lease termination if Delivery not achieved by January 1, 2022; early termination right if required SNDAs not provided within 60 days of Effective Date; early termination right if Recognition Agreement not provided within 60 days; Go-Dark termination right if Tenant does not open for business as a Target store within 12 months of Rent Commencement, or if Tenant goes dark for 12 consecutive months (subject to liquidated damages of $1,795,620 for failure to open).

remedies.description: Rent abates or reduces to 2% of Gross Sales if Best Buy closure exceeds 180 consecutive days or less than 65% of GLA is open
Tenant may terminate after 1 year if co-tenancy condition is not remedied within 2 years

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Commencement Date also conditioned on Best Buy (30,000 sf min) and Dick's Sporting Goods (45,000 sf min) opening for business per Â§4.3(2). Replacement tenant provisions not fully reviewed.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the lease if the Best Buy co-tenancy condition (180+ day closure or <65% GLA occupied) is not remedied within 2 years, exercisable after 1 year of the condition.
Tenant termination right under Â§4.4/Â§4.5 if Landlord's Construction Work is not timely commenced or completed.

extraction.verbatim_language: Inducement Store (Best Buy): if closure exceeds 180 consecutive days or if less than 65% of gross leasable area is open, rent reductions apply (rent abates or reduces to 2% of Gross Sales); Tenant may terminate after 1 year if condition not remedied within 2 years. Commencement Date also conditioned on Best Buy (30,000 sf min) and Dick's Sporting Goods (45,000 sf min) opening for business per Â§4.3(2).

termination_rights.details: If the Lease Commencement Date does not occur within 120 days after the Estimated Turn-Over Date (plus Force Majeure/Tenant Delay days), Tenant may terminate by written notice, effective if commencement does not occur within 30 days of the notice.
If Landlord is unable to deliver the Leased Premises by the end of one year after Landlord's execution of the Lease, either party (including Tenant) may terminate upon 30 days written notice.
If a casualty occurs and Landlord estimates repairs will take more than one year, Tenant may terminate the Lease with 10 days notice under Rider 1 Â§14.

extraction.verbatim_language: If the Lease Commencement Date does not occur on or before 120 days following the Estimated Turn-Over Date (plus Force Majeure/Tenant Delay days), Tenant may terminate upon written notice (effective if commencement doesn't occur within 30 days of notice). Conversely, if delay is due to Existing Tenant's failure to vacate, Landlord may terminate upon similar written notice. Also, if Landlord is unable to deliver Leased Premises by the end of one (1) year after Landlord's execution of the Lease, either party may terminate upon 30 days written notice. Additionally, casualty-related termination rights exist under Rider 1 Â§14 (if Landlord estimates repairs will take more than 1 year, Tenant may terminate with 10 days notice).

remedies.description: Tenant may pay Alternate Rent, defined as 50% of Base Rent and 100% of Additional Charges
Tenant may close (Permitted Closure) per Section 2.3 and related definitions

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Opening Co-Tenancy requires at least 2 of the Named Co-Tenants open; Continuing Co-Tenancy Condition requires minimum 200,000 SF of other tenants operating. Replacement tenant rules not fully detailed in reviewed excerpts.

extraction.verbatim_language: Opening Co-Tenancy: at least 2 of Named Co-Tenants (Buy Buy Baby, Home Goods, A.I. Friedman, Kohl's) must be open. Continuing Co-Tenancy Condition: minimum 200,000 sf of other tenants must be operating. Upon failure, Tenant may pay Alternate Rent (defined as 50% of Base Rent and 100% of Additional Charges) and/or close (Permitted Closure) per Section 2.3 and related definitions.

termination_rights.details: Either party may terminate if Tenant fails to obtain the Initial Construction Building Permit by the Approval Date, with sums paid by Tenant refunded (Â§5B).
termination_rights.termination_fee: None; sums paid by Tenant are refunded

termination_rights.details: Casualty termination rights (Â§14A): Landlord may terminate on 90 days' notice if Premises are 70%+ damaged during the last 18 months of the Term; either party may terminate if repairs cannot be completed within 365 days per Landlord's Repair Notice, if Landlord fails to complete repairs within 60 days after its estimated completion date, or if repairs remain incomplete after 18 months.

extraction.verbatim_language: Not a discretionary kick-out, but conditional termination rights tied to (1) Tenant's failure to obtain the Initial Construction Building Permit by the Approval Date (Â§5B) â either party may terminate, with sums paid by Tenant refunded; and (2) casualty/fire scenarios (Â§14A): Landlord may terminate if Premises are 70%+ damaged/destroyed during the last 18 months of the Term (90-day notice); either party may terminate if repairs cannot be completed within 365 days per Landlord's Repair Notice, or if Landlord fails to complete repairs within 60 days after its own estimated completion date, or if repairs remain incomplete after 18 months.

tenant_name: V NAIL BAR AND LASH INC

termination_rights.details: Condition precedent right: if Landlord fails to obtain the Ulta waiver (Exhibit H) within 15 days after the Date of Execution, either party may terminate the Lease by written notice, effective not less than 15 days after the non-terminating party's receipt, with the Security Deposit and prepaid rent returned to Tenant.
termination_rights.termination_fee: None; Security Deposit and prepaid rent returned to Tenant

extraction.verbatim_language: Â§4.E â Condition Precedent termination right: If Landlord fails to obtain the Ulta waiver (Exhibit H) within 15 days after Date of Execution, either party may terminate the Lease by written notice (termination effective not less than 15 days after non-terminating party's receipt), with Security Deposit and prepaid rent returned to Tenant.

remedies.description: Tenant may cease operations and continue paying full Rent.
Tenant may remain open and pay Alternative Rent of 5% of prior month's Gross Sales, capped at the monthly Minimum Rent then due.

If the Failure continues for 12+ months, Tenant may terminate within 60 days of the 12-month anniversary; if not exercised, Tenant reverts to full Rent. Upon termination, Tenant recovers unamortized TI costs straight-lined over the initial Lease Term.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: A successor to Target may satisfy the co-tenancy requirement; full replacement-tenant criteria not fully detailed in excerpted text. Co-Tenancy Failure defined as Target not open for 30+ consecutive days. If termination not exercised within the 60-day window, Tenant reverts to paying full Rent.

termination_rights.details: If Target (or successor) remains closed for 12+ months, Tenant may terminate the Lease within 60 days of the 12-month anniversary and recover unamortized TI costs (straight-line over the initial Lease Term).
termination_rights.termination_fee: Landlord pays Tenant unamortized TI costs on a straight-line basis over the initial Lease Term

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate if Gross Sales fall below $900,000 in Lease Year 5; referenced only in the file summary, full operative language and procedure not present in the reviewed excerpt.

extraction.verbatim_language: Co-Tenancy Requirement: Target must remain open and operating (or a successor). Co-Tenancy Failure = Target not open for 30+ consecutive days. Upon Failure, Tenant may: (a) cease operations and continue paying full Rent, or (b) remain open and pay Alternative Rent of 5% of prior month's Gross Sales (capped at the monthly Minimum Rent then due). If the Failure continues for 12+ months, Tenant may terminate the Lease within 60 days of such 12-month anniversary; if not exercised, Tenant reverts to paying full Rent. Upon termination, Tenant is entitled to recover unamortized TI costs on a straight-line basis over the initial Lease Term.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if gross sales during months 49-60 of the Relocation Premises Term do not meet a $900,000 threshold, upon payment of a $50,000 termination fee.
termination_rights.measure_period: months 49-60 of the Relocation Premises Term

extraction.verbatim_language: Gross Sales Termination Right: if Tenant's gross sales do not meet a $900,000 threshold during months 49-60 of the Relocation Premises Term, Tenant may terminate the Lease upon payment of a $50,000 termination fee.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: square footage of Rentable Area of the Shopping Center (excluding Anchor Tenant space and Building III)

remedies.description: Tenant may pay 50% of Monthly Base Rent, CAM, Insurance and Taxes (Ongoing Co-Tenancy Alternate Rent) retroactive to date of violation.

If violation continues 365 days or longer, Tenant may terminate upon 180 days' prior written notice, delivered within 30 days after expiration of the 365-day period; failure to timely terminate is an irrevocable waiver as to that specific violation (not future violations).

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Qualified Replacement = one or more national or regional operators occupying at least 80% of the square feet of the Anchor Tenant being replaced, operating first-class and consistent in quality/use with a majority of Shopping Center tenants and other first-class shopping centers in Westchester County, NY; may be a combination of replacements collectively satisfying the 80% threshold. Termination notice must be delivered within 30 days after expiration of the 365-day period, time of the essence; failure to timely terminate = irrevocable waiver as to that specific violation only.

termination_rights.details: If a Co-Tenancy Violation (fewer than 2 of Whole Foods/Target/Kohl's open or shopping center occupancy below 65%) continues for 365 days or longer, Tenant may terminate upon 180 days' prior written notice delivered within 30 days after the 365-day period expires.

Late Delivery Termination: if the Delivery Date does not occur within 120 days of the Anticipated Delivery Date (by June 16, 2026, or July 1, 2026 if extended), Tenant may terminate upon written notice prior to the actual Delivery Date, with Landlord reimbursing Tenant's reasonable actual out-of-pocket expenses up to $30,000 (Â§2.03).
termination_rights.termination_fee: Landlord reimburses Tenant's reasonable actual out-of-pocket expenses up to $30,000

termination_rights.details: Required Permits termination: if Required Permits are not issued within 180 days of Tenant's initial application and Landlord elects not to pursue them on Tenant's behalf, either party may terminate upon 10 days' prior written notice before permit issuance (Â§2.06(iv)).
Exclusive Use violation termination right: Tenant may terminate if an Exclusive Use violation continues for 180 days, extended to 365 days under the Rogue Tenant provisions (Â§27.02/27.03).
termination_rights.measure_period: 180-day violation, extended to 365 days for Rogue Tenants

extraction.verbatim_language: 'Co-Tenancy Violation' if: (i) less than two (2) of the named Anchor Tenants (Whole Foods, Target, Kohl's) or their Qualified Replacement(s) are open and operating, and/or (ii) less than sixty-five percent (65%) of the square footage of Rentable Area of the Shopping Center (excluding Anchor Tenant space and Building III) is leased by tenants open and operating. Remedy: Tenant may pay 50% of Monthly Base Rent, CAM, Insurance and Taxes (Ongoing Co-Tenancy Alternate Rent) retroactive to date of violation. If violation continues 365 days or longer, Tenant may terminate upon 180 days' prior written notice (Co-Tenancy Termination Notice), delivered within 30 days after expiration of the 365-day period, time being of the essence; failure to timely terminate = irrevocable waiver as to that specific violation (not future violations).

remedies.description: 50% abatement of Minimum Rent if either Co-Tenant is not open on the Rent Commencement Date or vacates/ceases operations for more than 30 consecutive days and does not reopen within 90 days; continues until the Co-Tenant or a suitable replacement (occupying at least 75% of the Co-Tenant's space) reopens.
If the co-tenancy failure is not satisfied after 365 days, Tenant may terminate the Lease or resume paying full rent.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Both named Co-Tenants (Target Corporation and Home Depot U.S.A., Inc.) must be open; failure triggered if either is not open on the Rent Commencement Date or vacates/ceases operations for more than 30 consecutive days without reopening within 90 days. A suitable replacement tenant opening in at least 75% of the vacating Co-Tenant's space satisfies the requirement. If failure persists 365 days, Tenant must elect to terminate or resume full rent.

termination_rights.details: Tenant may terminate the Lease if the co-tenancy failure (Target or Home Depot vacancy without suitable replacement) is not satisfied within 365 days; otherwise Tenant resumes paying full rent.

extraction.verbatim_language: Co-Tenants: Target Corporation and Home Depot U.S.A., Inc. If either Co-Tenant is not open on the Rent Commencement Date, or vacates/ceases operations for more than 30 consecutive days and does not reopen within 90 days, Tenant is entitled to a 50% abatement of Minimum Rent, continuing until the Co-Tenant (or a suitable replacement occupying at least 75% of the Co-Tenant's space) reopens. If not satisfied after 365 days, Tenant may terminate the Lease or resume paying full rent.

Lease Profile lists a Termination (TRM) option effective 2/28/2027 (coincides with lease expiration date), 0 SF/0 term/rate - likely reflects the natural lease-end rather than an early kick-out right; full text not reviewed.

co_tenancy.occupancy_basis: ground floor GLA (plus 9 of 18 Qualifying Tenants)

remedies.description: Tenant may pay 2% Alternate Rent (of Gross Sales) during opening periods if co-tenancy conditions are not satisfied.

co_tenancy.conditions_note: Three-tier structure: Construction Co-Tenancy requires 9 of 18 Qualifying Tenants plus 50% ground floor GLA; Opening Co-Tenancy requires 9 Qualifying Tenants plus 65% ground floor GLA; On-Going Co-Tenancy requires 9 Qualifying Tenants plus 70% ground floor GLA. Replacement tenant rules not detailed in excerpts reviewed - confirm against full lease text (Section 2.F cross-reference regarding replacement tenant reliance).

termination_rights.details: A Recapture Event (landlord recapture, not a tenant-held right per se) is triggered if Tenant fails to open for business within 270 days after the Commencement Date under Section 21.

Tenant held early cancellation rights during the Feasibility Period (Sections 6.A, 6.B, 6.D), which were waived per the First Amendment dated 12/17/2013 and Tenant's January 17, 2014 notice letter; right is no longer available.

extraction.verbatim_language: Construction Co-Tenancy requires 9 of 18 Qualifying Tenants plus 50% ground floor GLA; Opening Co-Tenancy requires 9 Qualifying Tenants plus 65% ground floor GLA; On-Going Co-Tenancy requires 9 Qualifying Tenants plus 70% ground floor GLA. Tenant may pay 2% Alternate Rent (of Gross Sales) during opening periods if co-tenancy conditions are not satisfied.
